summary: Pseudonymous Italian author of the Neapolitan Novels series (My Brilliant Friend, The Story of a New Name, Those Who Leave and Those Who Stay, The Story of the Lost Child). Also wrote The Days of Abandonment. Possibly the translator Anita Raja.

works.name: Neapolitan Novels
works.description: Four-novel series about Lenu (Elena Greco) and Lila (Raffaella Cerullo) growing up in Naples.

clues.clue: Series ends with delivery of dolls Tina and Nu that the girls threw into Don Achille's basement as children
examples: closed the final novel with the delivery of a package containing the dolls Tina and Nu

clues.clue: Girls throw each other's dolls into the basement of loan shark Don Achille
examples: two girls throw each other's dolls into the basement of the loan shark Don Achille

clues.clue: Lila experiences 'dissolving margins/boundaries' — the outlines of things and people disappear
examples: a woman first feels 'the outlines of things and people' disappear, a sensation she calls 'dissolving margins'

examples: a woman notices that the shoes she gifted her fiance are being worn by another man

works.name: The Days of Abandonment
works.description: Novel about Olga's breakdown after her husband leaves.

clues.clue: Opens: 'One April afternoon, right after lunch, my husband announced that he wanted to leave me'; Olga's mental breakdown

works.name: General / Identity

clues.clue: Memoir Frantumaglia describes growing up as child of a seamstress
examples: This author described growing up as the child of a seamstress in the memoir Frantumaglia

comprehensive_summary:
Elena Ferrante is a pseudonymous Italian author best known for the four Neapolitan Novels: My Brilliant Friend, The Story of a New Name, Those Who Leave and Those Who Stay, and The Story of the Lost Child. The series follows the lifelong friendship of Lenu (Elena Greco), the narrator, and her brilliant friend Lila (Raffaella Cerullo) growing up in a poor Naples neighborhood.

As children, the girls throw each other's dolls (Tina and Nu) into the basement of the loan shark Don Achille. Lila experiences 'dissolving margins' where the outlines of things disappear. She designs shoes, marries Stefano (Don Achille's son), and notices at her wedding that Marcello Solara is wearing the shoes she designed. Lila's father once threw her through a glass window for secretly taking the middle school exam. The series ends with Lenu receiving the dolls Tina and Nu in the mail.

Ferrante also wrote The Days of Abandonment about Olga's mental breakdown after her husband leaves. In 2016, Claudio Gatti 'unmasked' Ferrante as possibly being the translator Anita Raja; textual analysis has also pointed to Raja's husband Domenico Starnone. All English translations are by Ann Goldstein.
